purpose: Estimate the probability of a large (20%+) G10 FX move given current rate divergence conditions, replacing prior ad-hoc estimates with formal Bayesian updating
components: Beta-Binomial updating with ~6 hits in ~10 qualifying episodes
Sensitivity analysis across skeptical (35%), baseline (40%), and informative (45%) priors
Episode identification criteria for qualifying historical events
current_assessment: Posterior of 35-50% replaces original 61% estimate. Remarkably robust to prior choice — data-driven. However, wide CI constrains use for precise position sizing. Episode identification problem (n=7 vs n=15) is the primary residual uncertainty, shifting posterior by ±5pp.
limitations: 95% CI [21%, 61%] too wide for precise position sizing
Episode identification problem shifts posterior by ±5pp
Reference distribution non-stationarity may further reduce signal from extreme to moderate
Does not incorporate regime-specific adjustments for post-2008 structural changes

purpose: Determine which of the program's ~50-70 cumulative claims represent genuine phenomena vs. statistical artifacts from repeated testing on the same data
components: Bonferroni/HLZ adjustment for correlated tests requiring t≥3.8
Systematic review of iter_0006-0009 testable claims
Classification of survivors: UIP failure (t>5), carry fat tails (t>10), regime-conditional rate-FX (F=7.2), PCA collapse (mechanically verifiable)
Identification of non-survivors with specific test statistics
current_assessment: The single most important meta-finding: the program has identified a small number of genuine phenomena surrounded by a much larger body of suggestive-but-inconclusive findings. Only 3-4 claims survive the threshold.
limitations: Appropriate correlation structure for test adjustment is debatable
Threshold sensitivity around t=3.8 boundary
Some claims may be borderline and sensitive to threshold choice
New data could promote moderate-confidence findings above threshold

purpose: Monitor commodity-currency FX positioning opportunities by tracking alignment between terms-of-trade changes and rate differential changes
components: Binary sign of ΔToT (commodity terms of trade change direction)
Binary sign of Δrate differential (rate spread change direction)
March 2026 readings: JPY/EUR aligned bullish, NOK/AUD aligned bearish, CAD conflicting
Historical validation showing 2-3x larger FX moves during aligned concordance
current_assessment: Provides useful directional signal with specific current readings. Aligned concordance historically coincided with 2-3x larger FX moves. Binary construction needs augmentation with magnitude information and formal backtesting.
limitations: Binary construction discards magnitude information
Not formally backtested with transaction costs
Biofuel-energy-agriculture linkage (WTI-corn r=0.55-0.65) is a blind spot
Limited to commodity-exporter currencies

purpose: Real-time monitoring of carry unwind progression to distinguish contained vs. systemic events and inform dynamic hedging decisions
components: Phase 2: Short-end rate repricing (expected within 2-3 weeks of Phase 1)
Phase 4: Credit widening (conditional on credit cycle turn)
Decision rule: Phase 1 without Phase 2 in 2-3 weeks implies contained unwind
current_assessment: 71% fidelity across 7 major episodes. Genuinely useful for real-time decision-making during unwind events. Phase 4 activation conditional on credit cycle provides severity discrimination.
limitations: 2015 SNB compressed sequencing to hours
2020 Covid scrambled ordering entirely
Best suited for endogenous/positioning-driven unwinds, not exogenous shocks
Phase transitions may be too fast for institutional portfolio adjustment

purpose: Estimate conditional crisis probability from the joint configuration of multiple vulnerability dimensions, replacing marginal statistics with a proper multivariate framework
components: Copula specification options: Gaussian, Student-t, Frank
current_assessment: Conditional crisis probability ~25-40% within 12 months. Analytically superior to marginal statistics but fragile to copula specification (Gaussian 30-45%, Student-t 35-50%, Frank 20-35%). Should replace 61% compressed spring estimate as working number with ±10pp acknowledged.
limitations: Only n=4-6 effective episodes after clustering
±10pp sensitivity to copula choice
Marginal distribution estimation itself uncertain
Copula stationarity assumption questionable across structural breaks

purpose: Decompose carry returns to reveal hidden factor exposures and assess genuine diversification value for specific investor portfolios
components: Portfolio-specific assessment based on existing factor exposures
current_assessment: Most practically consequential insight for portfolio construction. For USD-based investors already overweight equities and credit, carry is redundant. For non-USD investors (GPIF, Norwegian SWF), carry may genuinely diversify via negative dollar beta.
limitations: Factor loading estimates lack confidence intervals
No regime conditioning on factor loadings
Ecological fallacy: conclusion is portfolio-specific, not universal
Time-varying dollar beta not formally modeled

purpose: Systematically compare current conditions to historical episodes across multiple structural dimensions to identify closest analogues and inform scenario analysis
current_assessment: Current episode scores 6/8 match with 2006-07, the highest similarity. Key structural differences (swap lines, microstructure) suggest modern episodes are faster, sharper, shorter. Systematic and not cherry-picked per debate assessment.
limitations: Equal weighting of dimensions is an untested assumption
n=1 for each historical episode limits statistical inference
Post-2008 structural changes may invalidate pre-2008 analogues
Microstructure impact assessment is ambiguous
